title: Ability to backdate task completion date
excerpt: Often it’s not possible to ‘live’ in Monday.com, so tasks that have actually been completed by a certain date aren’t flagged as ‘Done’ in Monday.com until several days later, resulting int he the task appearing to have b…

posts.author: pswal (Peter Wallace)
posts.cookedHtml: Often it’s not possible to ‘live’ in Monday.com, so tasks that have actually been completed by a certain date aren’t flagged as ‘Done’ in Monday.com until several days later, resulting int he the task appearing to have been done AFTER the completion date. Simialrly, when first using Monday.com and wanting to enter historical completed projects or tasks, they all appear to be be ‘done/completed’ on the same day as entered due to the timestamp feature. Can we have a way to amend the task completion date please.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Peter,

Thanks for your request!

To clarify, are you currently using the last updated column to track the task completion date? If this is the case, can I ask if you can instead use a standard date column so that you can easily adjust the completion date? Feel free to elaborate further on your situation if I’ve misunderstood

posts.author: Cholloway (Christine Holloway)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Bianca,

Not OP, but I have a similar question, and we use a standard date column. Once you hit “done” on a task, it says “completed x days past the due date” in the date column, which is great, but like Peter says above, if you update tasks that were completed on time the day after, it shows up as late. And, if you’re updating historical imported data (like we are), then things are showing up as months late.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Christine

Thanks for raising this.

I understand the issue here, however because the date is being set based on date/time the status column is triggered, it makes sense that if you set the status after the completion date, the result is inaccurate. I understand the need for a backdating functionality, so I am happy to share this feedback with our team internally

posts.author: JillianEG (jillian.hepinstall@evergreen.partners)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am running into the same issue currently. We use the standard Timeline column, along with the Completion Date and Completion Status. While we can get the Completion Status to show accurately, the Timeline column continues to show the Exclamation icon indicating it was done late (and saying it was done late when you hover over it).
It would be great if the Completion Date column could impact the Timeline column display.

posts.author: sberthelot (Stéphane BERTHELOT)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @BiancaT ,
We (I and other PMO colleagues) are continuously facing the same issue and we of course cannot keep track of dozen of projects every day only for having the good date recorded in the “done” column.

Can you prioritize this modification please ?
It would greatly help to be able to backdate Status columns !

posts.author: TFaust (Timo Faust)
posts.cookedHtml:
Any updates on this?

Managing multiple Projects it is really hard to make sure that every “done” status is set on the actual day.

Often times I will check if the colleagues marked their progress at the end of the week and have to backtrack their and some of my own status completions that I just could not type in due to travel time, sick days, etc.

posts.author: sberthelot (Stéphane BERTHELOT)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @BiancaT ,

Can we have any update on this ?
This is really a showstopper for realcase Project Management, and is ongoing since almost 3 years !
There should be a way, even with specific automation or AI to modify the “internal” date column linked to Status modification.

Thanks for your feedback.
